alerts.title: US and Iran trade new strikes: Sirik Island hit, IRGC claims attack on US air base
alerts.summary: Reuters, NBC, BBC and Anadolu report a fresh kinetic exchange in which US forces struck Iranian drone, radar and missile sites — including a reported attack on Sirik Island near the Strait of Hormuz (~26.7°N, 56.3°E) — and Iran's IRGC retaliated with a missile strike on a US air base. The Jerusalem Post adds Kuwait simultaneously defended against incoming drones and missiles, marking a clear breakdown of the prior ceasefire framework.

alerts.title: Israeli strike in Beirut reportedly kills IRGC Imam Hossein Division missile commander
alerts.summary: Long War Journal reports an Israeli airstrike in Beirut (~34.11°N, 35.45°E) targeted and reportedly killed the missile commander of the IRGC's Imam Hossein Division. The decapitation strike against a senior Iranian officer on Lebanese soil represents a direct escalation against IRGC command and risks broadening Hezbollah-Iran retaliation against Israeli and US targets.

alerts.title: Hezbollah suicide drone hits IDF base at Beit Hillel, serious injuries reported
alerts.summary: ME Spectator (~42K views) reports a Hezbollah suicide drone impacted an IDF base in Beit Hillel in Northern Israel, with serious injuries at the scene. The strike reinforces a pattern of Hezbollah FPV/loitering munitions inflicting growing IDF casualties as Israeli forces push deeper into southern Lebanon following the Beaufort Castle seizure.

alerts.title: US KC-135 tanker returns to UK with heavy shrapnel damage, possibly struck at Ben Gurion
alerts.summary: ME Spectator (~40K views) reports a US KC-135 aerial refueling tanker (tail 63-8028), previously stationed in Tel Aviv during the war, was observed returning to the UK with heavy shrapnel damage and likely out of service. The channel assesses the aircraft was most likely hit on the ground at Ben Gurion Airport by a cluster or airburst warhead, or possibly by an Iranian SAM — implying a previously undisclosed loss of a US strategic asset.

alerts.title: Kazakhstan offers to hold Iran's enriched uranium stockpile, IAEA chief says
alerts.summary: Financial Times, Kurdistan24 and Shafaq News report IAEA Director General Grossi has disclosed that Kazakhstan is willing to take custody of Iran's stockpile of 60% enriched uranium. With breakout time still assessed at ~1-2 weeks and Iran signaling it will amend the US draft text rather than accept it, the Astana option represents the first concrete third-party off-ramp to emerge in the nuclear talks.

analysis_note: Threat level remains at 9/10 with active US-Iran strike exchanges across the Gulf, Israeli decapitation strikes against IRGC commanders in Beirut, and Hezbollah drones inflicting IDF casualties — even as a Kazakhstan-mediated nuclear off-ramp tentatively emerges.
